pages.raw_text:
At 1880 a. m. September 10 the
first electric train will leave Spo-
kane for Moscow, Idaho, over the
new Moscow extension of the In
land Empire system. This train
will arrive in Moscow at 1 a. m.
and its coming will be made the
occasion of great rejoicing in the
Idaho city.


On this date the Inland will put
into operation it's first schedule to
Moscow, with two trains each way
daily. The cars will leave Spokane
at 1880 a. m. and $3.50 p. M, ar
irving in Moscow respectively at
IL a. m. and p. m. From Moscow
the cars will leave for Spokane at
1885 a. m. and 1910 noon, ar
irving in Spokane respectively at
1905 a. m. and 4:10 p. III. This
schedule will be as minutes faster
than the present service on the
Northern Pacific, which has a
somewhat longer route. The dis
lance to Moscow over the Inland
is 92 miles. A two and half cent
rate will be given or $3.50 for one
way as against rate Of $2.85 for
one way on the steam road.


AS soon as the roadbed is in
shape a new schedule will be put
into effect in which the running
time of the first schedule will be
shortened by at least half an hour.
